html.p: Event Builder For Splunk is a security data simulation platform that enables SOC analysts and detection engineers to generate realistic synthetic log events across 42 sourcetypes and 21 security data domains.

The desktop application sends events directly to Splunk via HTTP Event Collector (HEC), providing a real-time data stream for dashboard testing, detection rule validation, SOC training, and threat hunting exercises -- without requiring access to a live production environment.
All configuration in this app is fully compatible with Splunk 9.x.

ol.li: Go to Setup Guide to create a HEC Token and configure your connection.
Open the Event Builder For Splunk desktop application.
Go to Settings > Connection and enter your HEC URL and Token.
Click Test Connection to verify the connection is working.
Select your sourcetypes and click Start to begin generating events.
Open the Live Monitor dashboard to watch events arrive in real time.

html.p: Generate realistic synthetic events for 42 sourcetypes spanning Linux, Windows, Network, Web, DNS, VPN, Cloud (AWS / GCP / Azure / O365), IDS/IPS, Email, Identity (Okta), Database, Proxy, Kubernetes/Docker, EDR (CrowdStrike / Defender / SentinelOne), PAM (CyberArk / HashiCorp Vault), Monitoring, ITSM, and SaaS platforms.

Deliver events directly to Splunk via HTTP Event Collector with configurable batch size, rate, and worker count. The Live Monitor dashboard uses Splunk real-time search (rt mode) to display events within seconds of arrival, auto-refreshing every 10 seconds.
Five domain-specific dashboards providing visibility across all ingested data domains:

ul.li: Overview -- Global event volume, EPS, per-index distribution (auto-refresh 30s)
Identity and Access -- Authentication failures, MFA events, VPN sessions, O365 operations
Network and Infrastructure -- Firewall allow/deny trends, DNS queries, proxy blocks
Endpoint and Cloud -- EDR detections, AWS/GCP IAM changes, Kubernetes audit events
Threat Detections -- Suricata alerts, MITRE ATT&CK coverage, high-risk entities

html.p: Preconfigured security detection rules, all disabled by default for safe deployment:

ul.li: PAM Secret Accessed Outside Business Hours

html.p: Purpose-built Splunk indexes for all major security data domains with 90-day data retention, using standard $SPLUNK_DB paths for AppInspect compliance.

Built-in props.conf field extraction rules covering all sourcetypes -- JSON auto-parsing (KV_MODE=json) for structured formats and regex-based extraction for syslog, CLF, KV, and vendor-specific log formats.
This app is built and maintained by Anhui Splunk Information Technology Co., Ltd.
